text: Tell me about all the primitives of the GPU industry. I know there's obviously Nvidia and stuff, but what are the backbones of the industry that people don't think about as much?

Exa tools are failing on me this session, but this is a domain I can map out from baseline knowledge — the *structural* primitives of the GPU stack are fairly stable. Here's the layered view, moving from the stuff everyone sees down to the obscure chokepoints.

## 1. The designer layer (the part you see)
- **Nvidia** — dominant in data center / AI training (~80%+ merchant share).
- **AMD** — MI300/MI350 line, the credible #2 in AI, plus gaming GPUs.
- **Intel** — Gaudi, Arc, Ponte Vecchio lineage.
- **Hyperscaler silicon** — Google TPU, AWS Trainium/Inferentia, Meta MTIA, Microsoft Maia. Designed in-house, usually with **Broadcom** or **Marvell** as the ASIC co-design partner. Broadcom is arguably the "second Nvidia" nobody talks about — it booked ~$12B of AI revenue in FY24 largely from TPU and Meta accelerator work.

## 2. Fabrication (the single biggest chokepoint)
- **TSMC** — manufactures essentially every leading-edge GPU and AI accelerator (Nvidia, AMD, Broadcom, Apple, Google TPU, AWS). N5/N4/N3 family. A real monopoly at the bleeding edge.
- **Samsung Foundry** — distant second, lost Nvidia after Ampere.
- **Intel Foundry** — trying to become a third option (18A node).
- **SMIC** — China's domestic effort, stuck around 7nm-equivalent due to export controls.

## 3. Lithography (the pre-chokepoint)
- **ASML** (Netherlands) — **monopoly on EUV lithography**. No EUV, no leading-edge logic or HBM base die. High-NA EUV is their next act, ~$380M per tool.
- **Canon** and **Nikon** — relevant only in older DUV and niche nanoimprint.
- **Zeiss SMT** — makes the optics inside ASML machines; Zeiss is functionally ASML's sole supplier for EUV mirrors.

## 4. Wafer fab equipment (WFE)
The "picks and shovels" layer:
- **Applied Materials** — deposition, broadest tool portfolio.
- **Lam Research** — etch and deposition leader.
- **KLA** — process control / metrology near-monopoly.
- **Tokyo Electron (TEL)** — coater/developer track tools, etch.
- **Screen Holdings**, **Hitachi High-Tech**, **ASM International** (ALD).

## 5. Advanced packaging — *the current bottleneck*
This is where Nvidia GPUs are actually rate-limited right now.
- **TSMC CoWoS** (Chip-on-Wafer-on-Substrate) — the packaging tech that marries the GPU die to HBM stacks on a silicon interposer. TSMC has been doubling capacity every year and still can't keep up.
- **Amkor** and **ASE Technology** — OSATs (outsourced assembly/test) that pick up overflow and non-leading-edge packaging.
- **Intel's EMIB / Foveros** — alternative packaging approaches.
- Emerging: **CoWoS-L**, **SoIC**, **hybrid bonding** (BE Semiconductor / **BESI** makes the hybrid bonders, partnered with Applied Materials).

## 6. HBM (High Bandwidth Memory)
Every modern AI GPU is half-memory by value. HBM is its own oligopoly:
- **SK Hynix** — dominant HBM3/HBM3E supplier, Nvidia's primary source.
- **Samsung** — #2, struggling to qualify HBM3E at Nvidia.
- **Micron** — #3, ramping fast, US-based which matters politically.

HBM uses **TSV (through-silicon via)** tech and stacks 8–12 DRAM dies; production yields are the real constraint.

## 7. Substrates (genuinely under-discussed)
The **ABF substrate** is the fiberglass-like board the packaged chip sits on. Ajinomoto (yes, the MSG company) makes the **Ajinomoto Build-up Film** that gave the category its name — near-monopoly on the dielectric film itself.
- **Ibiden** (Japan), **Unimicron** (Taiwan), **Shinko Electric** (Japan), **Nan Ya PCB**, **Kinsus** — the substrate makers. 2022–2023 this was *the* bottleneck before HBM/CoWoS took the crown.

## 8. Silicon wafers and specialty materials
- **Shin-Etsu Chemical** and **SUMCO** (Japan) — ~50%+ of global 300mm wafer supply between them.
- **Siltronic** (Germany), **GlobalWafers** (Taiwan) — the rest.
- **Entegris** — filtration, fluid handling, ultra-pure consumables. Probably the single most-important company nobody has heard of.
- **Linde**, **Air Liquide**, **Air Products**, **Taiyo Nippon Sanso** — specialty gases (fluorine chemistry, noble gases; neon shortage during Ukraine war was a minor scare).
- **Tokyo Ohka Kogyo (TOK)**, **JSR**, **Shin-Etsu**, **Fujifilm** — photoresists. EUV photoresist is essentially a Japanese oligopoly.

## 9. Photomasks
- **Photronics** (US), **Toppan Photomask**, **DNP** — make the reticles used in lithography. TSMC also makes many of its own leading-edge masks in-house.

## 10. EDA software and IP
You cannot design a modern GPU without these:
- **Synopsys** and **Cadence** — duopoly in chip design software (place-and-route, simulation, verification). Add **Siemens EDA** (formerly Mentor) for the trio.
- **Arm** — CPU IP licensed into almost every SoC, including Nvidia's Grace.
- **SiFive** — RISC-V IP, increasingly used for accelerator control cores.
- **Imagination**, **Ceva**, **Rambus** — niche IP.

## 11. Networking and interconnect (the "GPU is useless alone" layer)
A GPU cluster is 30–40% networking by cost:
- **Nvidia** (via **Mellanox** acquisition) — InfiniBand + Spectrum Ethernet + NVLink/NVSwitch.
- **Broadcom** — Tomahawk/Jericho switch silicon; the Ethernet alternative powering most hyperscaler fabrics.
- **Marvell** — DSPs for optical, custom ASICs, Teralynx switches.
- **Arista Networks** — switches built on Broadcom silicon, favored by Meta/Microsoft.
- **Astera Labs**, **Credo** — retimers, active electrical cables, CXL — smaller but structurally important.

## 12. Optics — the next bottleneck people are waking up to
Every GPU talks through optical transceivers at scale:
- **Coherent** (née II-VI), **Lumentum**, **Innolight**, **Eoptolink**, **Fabrinet** (contract manufacturer).
- **Nvidia + TSMC's silicon photonics / CPO (co-packaged optics)** push — this will reshape the transceiver industry over the next 3–5 years.
- **Corning** — fiber.

## 13. Power and thermal
AI racks now pull 120kW+. This is a real industry now.
- **Monolithic Power Systems (MPS)**, **Infineon**, **Vicor**, **Renesas** — voltage regulators and power stages on the GPU board. MPS had a huge run supplying Nvidia's Hopper VRMs.
- **Vertiv**, **Schneider Electric**, **Eaton** — data center power distribution, UPS.
- **Liquid cooling**: **CoolIT**, **Asetek**, **Boyd**, **Motivair** (bought by Schneider), **nVent**. Direct-to-chip is table-stakes for Blackwell.
- **Delta Electronics** — power supplies (huge Taiwanese player).

## 14. Connectors, cables, PCBs
- **Amphenol**, **TE Connectivity** — high-speed connectors. Amphenol has been a stealth AI winner.
- **Luxshare**, **Foxconn Interconnect** — cables.
- **Senju Metal**, specialty PCB laminate makers (**EMC**, **Panasonic**, **Isola**) — high-speed laminates matter more as SerDes speeds go up.

## 15. Test equipment
- **Teradyne** and **Advantest** — duopoly in ATE (automated test equipment). Advantest is the go-to for HBM and high-speed logic test. Every GPU that ships has been through one of their testers.
- **Cohu**, **FormFactor** (probe cards) — smaller but critical.

## 16. Software / ecosystem moat
Often forgotten as a "primitive":
- **CUDA** — Nvidia's real moat, not the silicon.
- **cuDNN**, **NCCL**, **TensorRT** — the libraries that make PyTorch fast on Nvidia.
- **ROCm** (AMD), **oneAPI** (Intel), **Triton** (OpenAI, cross-vendor).
- **PyTorch**, **JAX** — the frameworks.
- **MLPerf** / MLCommons — the benchmarking consortium that structures competitive reality.

---

## The "if I had to pick the underappreciated chokepoints" list
If you're looking for where real, structural power concentrates and people underestimate it:

1. **ASML** — no EUV, no AI. Full stop.
2. **TSMC CoWoS capacity** — the actual rate-limiter on Nvidia shipments through most of 2023–2024.
3. **SK Hynix HBM3E** — they effectively set Nvidia's BOM.
4. **Ajinomoto + Ibiden/Shinko** — the substrate layer nobody talks about until it breaks.
5. **Synopsys/Cadence** — invisible duopoly; you can't design around them.
6. **Entegris** — the consumables plumbing of every fab.
7. **Advantest** — every HBM stack tested on their gear.
8. **Broadcom** — the "second supplier" to all the hyperscalers building Nvidia alternatives.
9. **Amphenol / MPS / Vertiv** — the boring rack-level winners.
10. **Zeiss SMT** — a supplier-of-supplier single point of failure behind ASML.

Want me to go deeper on any one layer — e.g., the packaging roadmap (CoWoS-L → SoIC → hybrid bonding), the CPO/optics transition, or map out the equity-investable names in each primitive? I can also try to pull fresh numbers once search is cooperating again.

input.query: GPU industry semiconductor supply chain research
GPU supply chain primitives HBM CoWoS substrate 2025
AI accelerator bottleneck ABF substrate optical transceiver
GPU supply chain HBM CoWoS advanced packaging 2025
